title: The Robbery

jokes.text: It seems like such an arbitrary, ridiculous thing... to just pick a finger and you show it to the person.
jokes.setup: Jerry recounts getting the finger from an angry driver
jokes.punchline: Breaking down the absurdity of the gesture itself
jokes.explanation: Classic Seinfeld observational humor - taking a common experience and deconstructing its absurdity. Jerry's voice shines through.

jokes.text: Someone shows me one of their fingers... and I'm supposed to feel bad. Is that the way it's supposed to work?
jokes.punchline: Questioning the basic logic of how offense is supposed to work
jokes.explanation: Good extension of the premise, questioning social conventions in Jerry's typical style.

jokes.text: I would feel worse if I got the toe than if I got the finger. It's not easy to give the toe. You gotta get the shoe off, the sock off and drive, get it up in the...
jokes.punchline: Escalating to the impracticality of giving someone 'the toe'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - taking the premise to an absurd but logical extreme. The physical comedy of imagining this scenario is excellent.

jokes.text: Look at that toe, buddy.
jokes.punchline: Jerry acting out the hypothetical toe gesture
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the toe premise, economical delivery makes it work.

jokes.text: I am the master packer.
jokes.punchline: Jerry's grandiose self-proclamation
jokes.explanation: Straightforward character moment showing Jerry's ego about mundane skills.

jokes.text: packing is like leading men into battle. You've got to know the strengths and weaknesses of every soldier. From a collapsible toothbrush to a pair of ordinary black socks.
jokes.punchline: Comparing packing to military strategy with specific examples
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of Jerry's packing obsession to military metaphors, very character-specific absurdity.

jokes.text: The hot water takes a while to come on. So the best thing to do... is to turn it on, do all your shopping... then come back and take a shower.
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous amount of time hot water takes
jokes.explanation: Good exaggeration of apartment problems, relatable urban living humor.

jokes.text: Deduct a minimum of two days off all expiration dates. No meat, no leftovers, no butter. And I cannot overstate this: No soft cheeses of any kind.
jokes.punchline: The escalating refrigerator warnings, ending with the emphatic cheese declaration
jokes.explanation: Good escalating structure with the emphatic soft cheese warning as a strong closer.

jokes.text: I'll eat out.
jokes.punchline: Elaine's simple, practical response
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response to George's overwrought warnings. Economical and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: regarding sexual activity. Strictly prohibited, but if you absolutely must, do us all a big favour... do it in the tub.
jokes.punchline: The uncomfortable sexual activity restriction
jokes.explanation: Perfectly uncomfortable George moment, escalating the apartment rules to an awkward extreme.

jokes.text: No. The waitress/actress. She just got some part... in some dinner theatre production of A Chorus Line.
jokes.punchline: The correction and dismissive description of dinner theater
jokes.explanation: Good character detail showing Elaine's disdain, the waitress/actress correction is a nice touch.

jokes.text: So now all day long she's walking around the apartment singing:
jokes.setup: Roommate got a part in A Chorus Line
jokes.punchline: Implied annoying singing (cut off by scene change)
jokes.explanation: Setup for annoying roommate behavior, works as character motivation.

jokes.text: Two bedrooms? Why do I need two bedrooms? I've got enough trouble maintaining activity in one.
jokes.punchline: Jerry's self-aware commentary on his social life
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jerry line - self-deprecating, economical, and revealing character insight about his lifestyle.

jokes.text: Because then I could move in here.
jokes.setup: George suggesting Jerry look at the apartment
jokes.punchline: George revealing his true motive
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing George's scheming nature, economical reveal of his ulterior motive.

jokes.text: The steam's been on for 10 years. No human can turn this off.
jokes.punchline: The impossible radiator situation
jokes.explanation: Good exaggeration of apartment problems, the 'no human' phrasing adds absurd emphasis.

jokes.text: Well, for me it's a step up. It's like moving from Iceland to Finland.
jokes.setup: Jerry pointing out George just said he wanted to live there
jokes.punchline: George's comparison to cold Nordic countries
jokes.explanation: Excellent analogy showing George's low standards, very quotable and character-specific.

jokes.text: I left the door open because I was gonna bring the spatula right back.
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous reason for leaving the door open
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the absurd chain of events leading to the robbery.

jokes.text: Well, I got caught up watching a soap opera. Bold and the Beautiful.
jokes.setup: Why she didn't return the spatula quickly
jokes.punchline: Getting distracted by a soap opera
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing how a trivial distraction led to disaster.

jokes.text: Because I spent my money on the Clapco D-29. It's the most impenetrable lock on the market today. It has only one design flaw: The door... must be closed!
jokes.punchline: The lock's obvious 'design flaw'
jokes.explanation: Excellent buildup and payoff structure. The dramatic pause and emphasis on the obvious flaw is perfectly executed.

jokes.text: In your way.
jokes.punchline: Jerry's deadpan correction/insult
jokes.explanation: Good economical insult, typical Jerry deadpan delivery correcting George's self-pity.

jokes.text: Boy, I hate the idea of somebody out there returning my calls.
jokes.setup: Listing stolen items including answering machine
jokes.punchline: Jerry's concern about the thief using his answering machine
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd concern - worrying about phone etiquette during a robbery. Very original and character-specific.

jokes.text: Do you ever find anything? - No.
jokes.punchline: Brutally honest answer about police effectiveness
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - the simple honest 'No' is brutally funny and realistic.

jokes.text: I'm living with Ethel Merman without the talent.
jokes.setup: Explaining why he wants Jerry's apartment
jokes.punchline: Comparing his mother to Ethel Merman minus the talent
jokes.explanation: Excellent analogy that's both specific and insulting. Very quotable and shows George's wit about his situation.

jokes.text: Now, unless they give the crook his copy... I don't really think we're gonna crack this case. Do you?
jokes.setup: Jerry's stand-up about police robbery reports
jokes.punchline: The absurd idea of giving the criminal a copy of the report
jokes.explanation: Great observational comedy taking bureaucratic processes to their logical absurd conclusion.

jokes.text: It's not like Batman, where there's three crooks in the city... and everybody pretty much knows who they are.
jokes.punchline: Contrasting real crime with Batman's simple world
jokes.explanation: Good pop culture reference that makes a valid point about crime fighting expectations vs reality.

jokes.text: Did you lose a Sony? It could be The Penguin. I think we can round him up. He's dressed like a penguin. We can find him. He's a penguin.
jokes.punchline: Imagining how easy it would be to catch themed criminals
jokes.explanation: Good extension of the Batman premise, though slightly overextended. The repetition works for emphasis.

jokes.text: What do you tip a wood guy?
jokes.setup: Learning about wood delivery for fireplace
jokes.punchline: Jerry's immediate concern about tipping etiquette
jokes.explanation: Very Jerry-like concern - immediately worrying about social protocols and tipping.

jokes.text: You don't tip a gardener. - You can tip a gardener. - You don't need a gardener.
jokes.punchline: Rapid escalation of gardener tipping debate
jokes.explanation: Good rapid-fire dialogue showing characters talking past each other about trivial concerns.

jokes.text: I like that.
jokes.punchline: George's understated approval while secretly plotting
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective understated reaction from George, economical character moment.

jokes.text: Give me... a hundred and fifty dollars.
jokes.setup: Jerry offering to help George by selling him the couch
jokes.punchline: The outrageous price demand
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Jerry's opportunism disguised as helping a friend.

jokes.text: I could go as high as... I don't know. Maybe... twenty dollars?
jokes.setup: Jerry asking what the couch is worth
jokes.punchline: George's hilariously low counteroffer
jokes.explanation: Perfect George moment showing his cheapness and negotiating style.

jokes.text: So you do want it. - I want it if you don't want it. - You just said you wanted it. - No, I'm saying if a situation arose... in which you didn't want it, I might take it.
jokes.setup: Discussion about who wants the apartment
jokes.punchline: George's convoluted logic about conditional wanting
jokes.explanation: Excellent circular dialogue showing George's overthinking and inability to be direct about his desires.

jokes.text: No, it hit the table. - So what? - Interference. You can't count that. Come on, are you crazy? The coin cannot touch anything. It affects it.
jokes.punchline: Absurd argument over coin flip rules
jokes.explanation: Good petty argument showing how they can complicate even simple decisions.

jokes.text: Why did I put out two? Why did I put out two?!
jokes.setup: George losing the odds/evens game for the apartment
jokes.punchline: George's anguished self-blame
jokes.explanation: Perfect George moment of self-torment over a random decision, very character-specific anguish.

jokes.text: Oh, by the way, I know about the stuff.
jokes.punchline: His hilariously bad undercover approach
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing George's incompetence at being subtle or clever.

jokes.text: See, he swallowed. See, the guy, he swallowed.
jokes.punchline: Reading too much into normal bodily functions
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of George's bad detective work, shows his tendency to over-analyze everything.

jokes.text: Did he call it? - Well, no, he didn't call it. - Bang. He has to call it. Case closed. Objection overruled.
jokes.setup: Jerry's stand-up about kid rules in adult court
jokes.punchline: Imagining a judge enforcing playground rules
jokes.explanation: Good observational premise about childhood vs adult rules, well-executed with legal terminology.

jokes.text: I don't know what in the hell it is, I look terrific in that mirror. I don't know if it's the tile or the lighting. I feel like Robert Wagner in there.
jokes.setup: Jerry talking about the apartment bathroom
jokes.punchline: The Robert Wagner comparison for how good he looks
jokes.explanation: Good vanity joke with a specific cultural reference that dates the show but works for the character.

jokes.text: They're closing in on 100, I'm saying to them, 'How can you eat that stuff?'
jokes.setup: Comparing his health-conscious diet to his grandparents
jokes.punchline: The irony of health advice to people living to 100
jokes.explanation: Great observational irony about health food trends vs proven longevity.

jokes.text: How can I ever have you over? You'll sit there moping. - I won't mope. - You're already moping.
jokes.punchline: Pointing out George is already exhibiting the behavior
jokes.explanation: Good character interaction showing Jerry's awareness of George's emotional patterns.

jokes.text: These are the guys who turned this place down. Can you believe it?
jokes.setup: Carol introducing her neighbors at the housewarming
jokes.punchline: Publicly highlighting Jerry and George's 'mistake'
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic irony and cringe moment, rubbing salt in their regret.

jokes.text: We're aware of the proximity to the park.
jokes.setup: Diane commenting on the great location near the park
jokes.punchline: Jerry's formally sarcastic response to the obvious
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan Jerry response, formally polite but clearly irritated. Very quotable phrasing.

jokes.text: I'm thinking of maybe buying a new TV... and smashing it over my head.
jokes.punchline: Absurd plan for self-punishment
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Jerry's frustration into absurd self-destructive fantasy.

tmdb_overview: Jerry is robbed after Kramer fails to close the apartment door. Elaine talks Jerry into looking at a fabulous apartment in the hopes that she can then have Jerry’s place. Things are further complicated when George decides he wants the new place too.
